headline: Peacock Scores High-Octane WWII Thriller in Exclusive Streaming Coup
lede: Guy Ritchie's star-powered war epic 'The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare' lands on Peacock next week, bringing pulse-pounding action to streaming audiences.
body:
Peacock is set to elevate its original content strategy with the exclusive streaming debut of Guy Ritchie's pulse-racing WWII action thriller, signaling a bold move in the competitive streaming landscape.

The film, featuring a heavyweight ensemble led by Henry Cavill and Alan Ritchson, promises to deliver Ritchie's signature kinetic storytelling with a historical backdrop. Ritchson, known for his physical prowess in action roles, continues to build momentum after his successful Jack Reacher series, bringing serious credibility to the wartime narrative.

Streaming exclusively on Peacock starting May 18, the movie represents a strategic acquisition for the NBCUniversal-owned platform. The deal highlights Peacock's aggressive approach to expanding its premium content library with high-profile, star-driven properties.

Ritchie's reputation for reinventing genre storytelling suggests this historical action thriller will offer more than standard war movie fare. The filmmaker's distinct visual style and narrative energy have consistently transformed genre expectations, from 'Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels' to 'Sherlock Holmes'.
key_facts: Starring Henry Cavill and Alan Ritchson
Part of Peacock's premium content strategy
